title: Wire safe_error + track_silent into Telegram error sites and god-mode logging
current_state: 7 Telegram error handlers in tools_telegram.py format raw exceptions into user-facing messages (lines 222, 250, 273, 300, 329, 379, 401). pi_agent.py:984 writes god-mode router error str(e) to logs/evolution.jsonl. pi_agent.py:420 returns raw exception text to user from _process_input wrapper. None of these go through redaction.
structural_problem: Privacy leakage. Stack traces and paths reach Telegram (a public push channel) and evolution.jsonl (a public log file). For god-mode this violates the privacy invariant (private computation must never touch public channels).
target_state: All 7 Telegram error sites use safe_error(e, audience='telegram'). pi_agent.py:984 either uses safe_error(e, audience='public_log') OR skips the log entirely when self.mode == 'god'. pi_agent.py:420 uses safe_error(e, audience='user'). Each swallow site additionally calls track_silent with a meaningful category.
files_affected: evolution.py (only if log_interaction signature changes â€” likely not)
tools/tools_memory.py (4 swallow sites flagged in audit: 514-516, 932, 953)
design_notes: Telegram handlers: replace 'f"[Pi] X error: {e}"' with 'f"[Pi] X error: {safe_error(e, audience=\'telegram\')}"'
All 7 Telegram error sites should also call track_silent('telegram.handler', e) so we can see if a handler is failing repeatedly

God-mode log isolation: in pi_agent.py:984 area, before calling evolution.log_interaction, check if self.mode == 'god'. If god, do NOT call (log to data/god_evolution.jsonl in private location instead). Public log stays clean.
_log_worker (pi_agent.py:336): change 'except Exception: pass' to 'except Exception as e: track_silent("logs.async_worker", e)'
append_turn swallow (pi_agent.py:436): same pattern with category 'logs.turn_append'
Audience choice matrix: Telegram=audience='telegram', evolution.jsonl=audience='public_log', returned to user=audience='user', internal logs/dev tools=audience='dev'
success_criteria: No Telegram error message contains a raw absolute path
No Telegram error message contains a key-like substring (sk-, eyJ, gsk_)
When self.mode == 'god', no entry is appended to logs/evolution.jsonl (asserted by test)
All 4 swallow sites in the audit now record via track_silent
Recent failures by category visible in system_introspect after a synthetic failure
verify.py PASS â€” all 734 existing tests + 2 new
risk_notes: Risk: redaction over-aggressive on legitimate Telegram error context (e.g. 'file <path> not found' becomes 'file <path> not found' â€” still useful but less specific). Mitigation: telegram audience preserves enough context for the user to act; if they need full detail they can check internal logs.
migration_plan: 1. Telegram: edit 7 sites in tools_telegram.py â€” wrap each 'f"...{e}"' with 'safe_error(e, audience=\'telegram\')' and add track_silent('telegram.handler', e) before bot.reply_to
2. pi_agent.py:336 (_log_worker): replace bare except with track_silent('logs.async_worker', e)
4. pi_agent.py:420 area (user-facing return): change str(e) to safe_error(e, audience='user')
5. pi_agent.py:984 (god-mode log path): add 'if self.mode == "god": skip public log; private log path' â€” guarded by ModeConfig.public_logging
6. tools_memory.py:514-516, 932, 953: add track_silent calls with specific categories
7. Add testing/test_error_redaction_wiring.py: simulate a Telegram exception path and assert redacted output
8. Add testing/test_god_log_isolation.py: simulate god-mode error path, assert logs/evolution.jsonl unchanged
test_plan: test_telegram_handler_redacts_path â€” patch agent.process_input to raise FileNotFoundError('/Users/ash/secret'); call handler; assert reply does not contain '/Users/ash'
test_telegram_handler_records_silent â€” same setup, assert track_silent was called with 'telegram.handler'
test_god_mode_skips_public_log â€” set self.mode='god'; trigger a router exception; assert logs/evolution.jsonl line count unchanged
test_log_worker_records_silent â€” patch fn to raise; queue work; drain queue; assert track_silent recorded 'logs.async_worker'
test_append_turn_swallow_records â€” patch open() to raise; call append_turn; assert track_silent recorded 'logs.turn_append'
test_memory_swallow_sites â€” patch supabase to raise; call relevant methods; assert track_silent recorded the correct categories
